💬 Genuine vs Suspicious Language

What Genuine Reviews Sound Like

"Pleasant 10 min walk along the sea front to the Water Bus. restaurants etc. Hotel was comfortable breakfast was good - quite a variety. Room aircon didn't work very well. Take mosquito repelant!..."
"Really lovely hotel. Stayed on the very top floor and were surprised by a Jacuzzi bath we didn't know we were getting! Staff were friendly and helpful and the included breakfast was great! Great locat..."
"Ett mycket bra hotell. Det som drog ner betyget var att vi fick ett rum under taksarna dr det endast var full sthjd i 80 av rummets yta...."
"We loved staying on the island of Lido! You need to take a water is from Venice to get there. From the train station, a boat ride takes 45 minutes but has beautiful views along the way. Hotel is an EA..."
"Lovely view out onto the lagoon. Excellent view. Staff were welcoming and helpful...."

What Suspicious Reviews Sound Like

"It ws just an over night but very good accommodations..."
"very nice!..."
"it was very good..."
"Everything was great!..."
"Do not stay here. It's awful...."

⚖️ The Verdict

Overall trustworthiness assessment:
0.2% of reviews show strong fraud indicators
Dominant pattern: Behavior (20,867 reviews affected)
  • Implement per-user daily review limits to prevent burst campaigns
  • Auto-cluster near-duplicate text to catch copy-paste fraud rings
  • Require verified stay confirmation before publishing 5-star reviews
